meaning: n.[亦作maneuvre] 策略,调动v.用策略,调动,演习,(敏捷地)操纵
examples: There was very little room to manoeuvre.
The chopper shot upward in a manoeuvre matched by the other pilot.
That will allow them to manoeuvre the satellite into the shuttle's cargo bay.
The manoeuvre will put the probe on a path to flyby Earth in October next year.
Reality, officials in Berlin say, will give the French president very little room for manoeuvre.
